steps.narrationText: Testing infrastructure locally prevents production misconfigurations. We verify that your workstation has KVM support, libvirt, QEMU, and cloud-init tools installed for virtual machine provisioning.
All prerequisites pass. We provision a minimal two-VM cluster on a virtual network -- a bridge node and a worker node -- with SSH access ready.
Cluster provisioning complete. We check that both VMs are running, with their assigned IP addresses, CPU allocation, and memory visible in the status table.
Both VMs are online. We verify SSH access to the bridge node by running a remote command. You'll see its hostname returned, confirming the tunnel works.
SSH confirmed. This time we pull full system details -- kernel version, architecture, and OS release -- to verify the VM environment matches expectations.
When testing is complete, we tear down the cluster. This destroys both VMs and frees all CPU, memory, and disk resources. You can reprovision at any time.
